## Reviewer's Guide Implements EPIC-7-QUALITY Phase 2 security hardening by adding centralized path validation and file I/O retry helpers, tightening error handling and logging in IPC, UI callbacks, and sticky state persistence, and introducing diagnostic counters while also applying minor UI code style/consistency refactors. #### Sequence diagram for sticky state write with path validation and IO retry ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Strategy as V12_002 participant PathValidation participant RetryHelper participant FileSystem as System.IO.File Strategy->>PathValidation: PathValidation-->>Strategy: validTempPath Strategy->>PathValidation: ValidateAndCanonicalize(backupPath, WriteBackupState) PathValidation-->>Strategy: validBackupPath Strategy->>RetryHelper: ExecuteWithRetry(File.WriteAllText(validTempPath, jsonWithChecksum), IsTransientIOError, WriteStateTempFile) RetryHelper->>FileSystem: WriteAllText(validTempPath, jsonWithChecksum) FileSystem-->>RetryHelper: [success or transient error] RetryHelper-->>Strategy: [completion] alt existing state file Strategy->>RetryHelper: ExecuteWithRetry(File.Copy(validStatePath, validBackupPath, overwrite), IsTransientIOError, BackupStateFile) RetryHelper->>FileSystem: Copy(validStatePath, validBackupPath) FileSystem-->>RetryHelper: [completion] end Strategy->>RetryHelper: ExecuteWithRetry(File.Delete(validStatePath), IsTransientIOError, DeleteOldStateFile) RetryHelper->>FileSystem: Delete(validStatePath) FileSystem-->>RetryHelper: [completion] Strategy->>RetryHelper: ExecuteWithRetry(File.Move(validTempPath, validStatePath), IsTransientIOError, MoveStateFile) RetryHelper->>FileSystem: Move(validTempPath, validStatePath) FileSystem-->>RetryHelper: [completion] Strategy-->>Strategy: _lastSnapshotTicks = UtcNow.Ticks ``` ### File-Level Changes | Change | Details | Files | | ------ | ------- | ----- | | Hardened file/state I/O with centralized path validation, retry logic, and improved error handling. |
  • Introduced PathValidation helper to canonicalize and sandbox file and directory paths under the NinjaTrader data directory, throwing SecurityException on traversal or invalid paths.
  • Added RetryHelper with exponential backoff for transient file I/O errors, plus retry diagnostics, and wired it into snapshot read/write, backup handling, and CSV/compliance log writes.
  • Updated sticky state read/write and rollback to use validated paths, retry logic, and more granular logging/counters for corruption, temp cleanup failures, and security violations.

>Code >

>
>Evidence >
> >
>The only retry catch is guarded by attempt < maxAttempts; therefore when attempt == maxAttempts,

> >``` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >### Issue description >`ExecuteWithRetry()` intends to log/increment failures after retries are exhausted, but its catch filter excludes the final attempt (`attempt < maxAttempts`). If the last attempt throws, the exception escapes immediately and the post-loop failure accounting/logging never executes. > >### Issue Context >This helper is used for critical state persistence and CSV logging paths; when retries are exhausted, observability and metrics are expected to capture the failure. > >### Fix Focus Areas >- src/V12_002.IO.RetryHelper.cs[36-111] > >### Suggested fix >Restructure the loop so the final failed attempt is handled: >- Validate arguments: `maxAttempts >= 1`, `baseDelayMs >= 0`, `operation != null`, `isRetryable != null`. >- Use a single catch for retryable exceptions and branch on attempt: > - If `attempt == maxAttempts`: increment failures, log, then rethrow. > - Else: increment attempts, backoff, continue. >- Remove the current post-loop `return operation();` block (itÔÇÖs only reachable for `maxAttempts <= 0`). >``` > Ôôÿ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools >

5. Rollback copy bypasses validation Ô£ô Resolved ­ƒÉ× Bug Ôø¿ Security
>
>Description >
> >
>RollbackToLastGoodState() introduces validBackupPath via PathValidation, but still performs
>File.Copy(backupPath, _stickyStatePath, ...) using the original unvalidated paths. This leaves a
>hardened-looking codepath that can still write using untrusted/non-canonical paths and bypasses the
>new retry/validation controls.

>
>Code >
> >[src/V12_002.StickyState.cs[R255-274]](https://github.com/malhitticrypto-debug/universal-or-strategy/pull/7/files#diff-b91c8d843c31d4dc5f5c2533f3533e125926780d9aad5c914c2b01cd45bdc1f6R255-R274) >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>The method now computes a validated canonical backup path, but the rollback copy operation still
>uses the original (non-validated) backupPath and _stickyStatePath, which defeats the intended
>hardening for the write step.
>
> > [src/V12_002.StickyState.cs[254-306]](https://github.com/malhitticrypto-debug/universal-or-strategy/blob/8fd9b0655e319dd6c3c8e9ee4fb9cf3b7a9160d8/src/V12_002.StickyState.cs/#L254-L306) >
>
>Agent prompt >

## Summary

This PR successfully implements file I/O security hardening through path validation and retry logic with exponential backoff. The implementation follows security best practices and integrates cleanly with the existing codebase.

**Key Improvements:**
- Ô£à Path traversal protection with canonicalization and sandbox validation
- Ô£à Transient I/O error handling with exponential backoff (50ms, 100ms, 200ms)
- Ô£à Comprehensive security exception handling with fail-fast behavior
- Ô£à Thread-safe diagnostic counters for retry operations
- Ô£à Proper integration across sticky state persistence, lifecycle, and UI modules

**Code Quality:**
- All new security modules are well-structured with clear documentation
- Error handling is comprehensive with appropriate logging
- The retry logic correctly identifies transient errors (file locks, sharing violations, permission issues)
- Path validation prevents directory traversal attacks and enforces sandbox boundaries

The changes are production-ready and significantly improve the robustness of file I/O operations in the trading strategy.

Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The new state persistence diagnostic counters (_statePersistenceFailures, _stateSecurityViolations, etc.) are currently never incremented; consider wiring them into the catch blocks in StickyState/Compliance so they provide meaningful telemetry instead of remaining unused.
- RetryHelper.IsTransientIOError treats UnauthorizedAccessException as retryable in all cases, which can mask permanent permission issues; consider narrowing this to known transient scenarios or letting callers decide when UnauthorizedAccess should be retried.
- RetryHelper.ExecuteWithRetry logs a final failure and then immediately calls the operation again outside the retry loop, which may perform a fourth attempt with side effects but without the retry guard; consider performing the last attempt inside the loop or rethrowing the last exception instead of re-invoking the operation.

## Overall Comments
- The new state persistence diagnostic counters (_statePersistenceFailures, _stateSecurityViolations, etc.) are currently never incremented; consider wiring them into the catch blocks in StickyState/Compliance so they provide meaningful telemetry instead of remaining unused.
- RetryHelper.IsTransientIOError treats UnauthorizedAccessException as retryable in all cases, which can mask permanent permission issues; consider narrowing this to known transient scenarios or letting callers decide when UnauthorizedAccess should be retried.
- RetryHelper.ExecuteWithRetry logs a final failure and then immediately calls the operation again outside the retry loop, which may perform a fourth attempt with side effects but without the retry guard; consider performing the last attempt inside the loop or rethrowing the last exception instead of re-invoking the operation.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1

<issue_to_address>
**issue (bug_risk):** Static RetryHelper cannot call instance Print, and the retry loop never reaches the failure logging block.

Within the static RetryHelper class, ExecuteWithRetry<T> calls Print(...) directly, but Print is an instance method on the Strategy, so this will not compile from a static context. YouÔÇÖll need to either use a static logger (e.g., Debug/Trace/static callback) or pass an Action<string> logger into ExecuteWithRetry.

Also, the code after the for-loop in ExecuteWithRetry<T> is never reached:
- On the last attempt (attempt == maxAttempts), any exception skips the catch filter and escapes the method.
- Therefore, Interlocked.Increment(ref _ioRetryFailures), the final Print, and the extra operation() call after the loop are all unreachable.

Consider either (a) looping for attempts 1..maxAttempts-1 and doing a final attempt with logging/counter update outside the loop, or (b) removing the post-loop block and adjusting the catch filter so the last attempt updates diagnostics before rethrowing. As written, failure diagnostics and the intended final non-retried attempt never occur.
</issue_to_address>

## Code Review

This pull request introduces comprehensive diagnostic counters, secure path validation, and exponential backoff retry logic for file I/O operations across several modules (Data, Lifecycle, StickyState, and Compliance). It also implements pool-based array renting for order submissions and improves error logging for UI callbacks. The review comments identify a critical path traversal vulnerability in the path validation logic, a potential memory leak in the order array pool due to uncleared references, and several missing null checks that could lead to background thread crashes. All review comments are valid and provide actionable feedback.

### Pull Request Overview

This PR is currently not up to standards according to Codacy, with 77 new issues introduced. While the PR intended to implement security hardening and centralized path validation, a critical acceptance criterion (TICKET-011: File I/O retry logic) appears to be entirely missing from the codebase despite being listed in the intent. 

Furthermore, there are no unit tests included to verify the new security constraints or error handling logic. A severe bug was identified in the order propagation logic where pooled arrays are passed to a method that iterates the full collection, which will lead to NullReferenceExceptions. The centralized path validation also contains a prefix-matching flaw that allows access to sibling directories with similar names.

#### About this PR
- The implementation of TICKET-011 (Retry Logic with exponential backoff) is missing. The PR description references a 'RetryHelper.cs' and updated logic in 'StickyState.cs', but these changes are not present in the diff.
- The PR lacks any new test files to verify the security hardening logic (PathValidation) or the updated error handling and diagnostic counters.



#### Test suggestions
- [ ] Verify that path traversal attempts (e.g., using '..') are blocked by PathValidation and throw a SecurityException.
- [ ] Test state persistence recovery when the JSON file is corrupted or formatted incorrectly.
- [ ] Verify that IPC server shutdown correctly closes all client sockets and handles 'stale' connections.
- [ ] Test exponential backoff retry logic during file lock contention (TICKET-011).
